description: Overview of the fundamental concepts of motor abilities: strength, endurance, speed, flexibility, and coordination.

questions.explanation: A motor ability is a physical property of the human body manifested in movement, which can be developed through training.

options.text: The resting state of the body
A physical property manifested in movement
The name of a training plan

questions.text: Which motor ability enables sustained physical performance over time?
questions.explanation: Endurance enables the athlete to perform continuously while resisting fatigue.

questions.text: What is the primary role of strength in movement?
questions.explanation: Strength is the muscle's ability to overcome or exert resistance.

questions.text: Flexibility is related to joint range of motion.
questions.explanation: True. Flexibility is the ability of joints and muscles to move through their full range of motion.

questions.text: What is speed as a motor ability?
questions.explanation: Speed is the ability to perform movements in the shortest possible time.

options.text: The rate at which movements are executed

questions.text: Which ability is responsible for the coordination of movements?
questions.explanation: Coordination ensures that different muscle groups work together in harmony, producing the desired movement pattern.

questions.text: Motor abilities can be developed through training.
questions.explanation: True. Although genetic traits play a role, motor abilities can be significantly improved through regular training.

questions.text: Which motor ability best characterises a football player's capacity to perform a sprint?
questions.explanation: A sprint is a maximal-speed run that primarily draws on speed as the motor ability.

questions.text: Endurance and strength are completely independent motor abilities.
questions.explanation: False. Motor abilities are interconnected; for example, strength forms the foundation of strength-endurance.

questions.text: Why is flexibility important for athletes?
questions.explanation: Adequate flexibility reduces injury risk and enables efficient, full-amplitude movement execution.

options.text: It reduces injury risk and increases range of motion
